name: Fritz Kaegi
bio: Fritz Kaegi is the incumbent Cook County Assessor and a candidate in the 2026 Democratic primary. The assessor's office oversees property assessment values used in county tax billing. (Source: scout ballot context, March 13, 2026)

jacket.note: County race — ILSBE filing, not FEC.

red_flags.label: Both candidates accuse each other of accepting questionable donations (Fox32 Feb
red_flags.detail: Both candidates accuse each other of accepting questionable donations (Fox32 Feb 2026 — mutual accusations, neither substantiated)

red_flags.label: Hynes campaign argues Kaegi's data errors caused chaotic assessment appeals and
red_flags.detail: Hynes campaign argues Kaegi's data errors caused chaotic assessment appeals and missed deadlines — contested claim

red_flags.label: Kaegi has loaned his own campaign $5.4M (debt obligation on ILSBE) — heavy self-
red_flags.detail: Kaegi has loaned his own campaign $5.4M (debt obligation on ILSBE) — heavy self-funding by a wealthy candidate

key_votes.bill: 2018: Defeated incumbent Joseph Berrios (implicated in pay-to-play) — ran on tra
key_votes.summary: 2018: Defeated incumbent Joseph Berrios (implicated in pay-to-play) — ran on transparency/anti-corruption platform

key_votes.bill: 2018-present: Implemented mass reassessment reforms using data-driven methods; U
key_votes.summary: 2018-present: Implemented mass reassessment reforms using data-driven methods; U of Chicago study (2024) found 'substantial progress in improving fairness of residential assessments'

key_votes.bill: 2022: Survived primary challenge from Kari Steele (MWRD President) 53.8% to 46.2
key_votes.summary: 2022: Survived primary challenge from Kari Steele (MWRD President) 53.8% to 46.2% — contested race reflecting industry pushback

key_votes.bill: 2022-2025: Launched Affordable Housing Program, automated senior/veteran exempti
key_votes.summary: 2022-2025: Launched Affordable Housing Program, automated senior/veteran exemption renewals, expanded field inspection staff

key_votes.bill: 2025: Cook County Assessor's Office released 2024 Chicago Reassessment Report sh
key_votes.summary: 2025: Cook County Assessor's Office released 2024 Chicago Reassessment Report showing equity improvements; expanded commercial valuation reform with PTAX Reform Group

key_votes.bill: Debts of $5.4M on committee suggest significant self-loan to campaign
key_votes.summary: Debts of $5.4M on committee suggest significant self-loan to campaign

career_history.highlight: Won on reform platform; overhauled assessment equity practices

policy_platform.position: Championed fair property assessment reform, reducing the longstanding 'regressivity' that over-taxed lower-value properties while undertaxing commercial real estate.
Created online appeal tracking, published assessment methodology, expanded data access. First assessor to publish detailed mass appraisal models.
Raised assessments on undervalued commercial and industrial properties; faced intense opposition from commercial real estate lobby.

social_pulse.summary: Positive among housing equity advocates and progressive media. Negative from commercial real estate and property tax appeal attorneys. Moderate name recognition overall.

trust_indicators.label: No confirmed corruption or ethics findings
